what: A new paradigm: 22 GB of CalCOFI, queryable from anywhere
title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere
text: CalCOFI sampling California Current since 1949 — three quarters century co-located biological (net-tow ichthyoplankton + zooplankton biomass) environmental (CTD-bottle) observations. recently, using record cross-domain analysis meant picking friction: Postgres server credentials, API account, downloading 22 GB CSV joining . doesn’t anymore. Every CalCOFI release now published versioned, public Apache Parquet Google Cloud Storage — DuckDB can query directly HTTPS. credentials. server. full download. DuckDB reads columns row groups query actually touches, runs engine : ask one cross-domain question — Pacific sardine larvae experience 2014–2016 marine heatwave? — answer single calcofi4r call, unpack makes call reproducible anyone environments.

what: One question, one call, 310 matched observations
title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere
text: cc_match_ichthyo_by_name() joins ichthyoplankton tows CTD-bottle measurements fly: temporal interval join (within max_time_hr) plus spatial ST_Distance_Sphere filter (within max_dist_km), one row per biological observation matched environmental value averaged window. Relaxed matching widens windows 5 km / 72 hr. row one net-haul observation sardine egg larva, joined nearest co-located CTD cast measured temperature. bio_* columns describe biology (bio_value standardized larval tally, bio_datetime / bio_lon / bio_lat + ), env_* columns describe matched temperature (env_value mean n_env bottle measurements within match window, env_depth_m mean sampling depth), dist_km / time_diff_hr realized offsets — well inside 5 km / 72 hr relaxed window.
code: library(calcofi4r) library(dplyr)  d <- cc_match_ichthyo_by_name(   scientific_name = "Sardinops sagax",   env_var         = "temperature",   date_min        = "2014-01-01",   date_max        = "2019-12-31",   relax_matching  = TRUE,   version         = "v2026.05.14")   # pin for archival reproducibility  dim(d) #> [1] 310  19 d |>   select(scientific_name, life_stage, bio_datetime,          bio_lon, bio_lat, bio_value,          env_value, env_depth_m, n_env,          dist_km, time_diff_hr) |>   head(6) #> # A tibble: 6 × 11 #>   scientific_name life_stage bio_datetime        bio_lon bio_lat bio_value #>   <chr>           <chr>      <dttm>                <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 Sardinops sagax larva      2019-02-12 21:54:00   -119.    33.8    112.   #> 2 Sardinops sagax larva      2015-02-05 07:04:00   -124.    37.6     88.2  #> 3 Sardinops sagax larva      2017-04-07 12:48:00   -123.    31.7      5.02 #> 4 Sardinops sagax egg        2014-04-08 06:38:00   -118.    33.9      0.6  #> 5 Sardinops sagax egg        2016-07-22 09:37:00   -119.    34.3    292.   #> 6 Sardinops sagax larva      2014-07-13 23:09:00   -120.    33.3      7.38 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: env_value <dbl>, env_depth_m <dbl>, n_env <dbl>, #> #   dist_km <dbl>, time_diff_hr <dbl>

title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere
text: Pacific sardine egg + larva observations across 2014–2019 (Q1–Q4), one point per net haul, coloured matched CTD-bottle temperature. 2014–2016 Northeast Pacific marine heatwave (Blob) strong El Niño — warmer reds dominate panels.
code: library(ggplot2) library(sf)  # California Current coastline from Natural Earth (already a Suggest for the pkg) coast <- rnaturalearth::ne_countries(   scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf",   country = c("United States of America", "Mexico"))  d_yr <- d |>   mutate(year = lubridate::year(bio_datetime)) |>   sf::st_as_sf(coords = c("bio_lon", "bio_lat"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)  ggplot() +   geom_sf(data = coast, fill = "gray92", colour = "gray70", linewidth = 0.2) +   geom_sf(data = d_yr,           aes(colour = env_value, shape = life_stage),           size = 1.6, alpha = 0.85) +   scale_colour_viridis_c(     option = "inferno", direction = 1, name = "Temp\n(°C)") +   scale_shape_manual(values = c(egg = 16, larva = 17), name = NULL) +   scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-124, -120)) +   coord_sf(xlim = c(-126, -117), ylim = c(30, 38.5), expand = FALSE) +   facet_wrap(~ year, ncol = 3) +   labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),         legend.position  = "right",         strip.text       = element_text(face = "bold"))

title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere
text: Standardized larval tally (log-scaled) versus matched bottle temperature. LOESS smoother life stage suggests heaviest hauls fall cooler 11–14 °C band — typical sardine spawning conditions — relatively observations warmest waters heatwave.
code: ggplot(d, aes(env_value, bio_value + 1, colour = life_stage)) +   geom_jitter(alpha = 0.5, width = 0.0, height = 0.05, size = 1.6) +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.9) +   scale_y_log10() +   scale_colour_manual(values = c(egg = "#1f78b4", larva = "#e31a1c"),                       name = NULL) +   labs(x = "Matched bottle temperature (°C)",        y = expression(paste("Standardized larval tally + 1  ",                             "(", log[10], ")"))) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),         legend.position  = "top")

what: The signal of the heatwave, year by year
title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere
text: Annual summary matched sardine ichthyoplankton + temperature. Annual mean matched temperature (top) number matched hauls (bottom). 2014–2016 sits multi-year mean — marine heatwave + 2015–16 El Niño — sardine hauls drop sharply period.
code: yearly <- d |>   mutate(year = lubridate::year(bio_datetime)) |>   group_by(year) |>   summarise(     n_obs        = n(),     n_egg        = sum(life_stage == "egg"),     n_larva      = sum(life_stage == "larva"),     mean_temp_C  = round(mean(env_value), 2),     range_temp_C = sprintf("%.1f – %.1f",                            min(env_value), max(env_value)),     median_tally = round(median(bio_value), 1),     .groups      = "drop")  knitr::kable(yearly,   caption = "Annual summary of matched sardine ichthyoplankton + temperature.") library(patchwork)  p1 <- ggplot(yearly, aes(year, mean_temp_C)) +   geom_col(fill = "#cb181d", width = 0.65) +   geom_hline(yintercept = mean(yearly$mean_temp_C),              linetype = "dashed", colour = "gray40") +   labs(x = NULL, y = "Mean matched\ntemp (°C)") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())  p2 <- yearly |>   tidyr::pivot_longer(c(n_egg, n_larva),                       names_to = "life_stage", values_to = "n") |>   mutate(life_stage = sub("n_", "", life_stage)) |>   ggplot(aes(year, n, fill = life_stage)) +   geom_col(position = "stack", width = 0.65) +   scale_fill_manual(values = c(egg = "#1f78b4", larva = "#e31a1c"),                     name = NULL) +   labs(x = "Year", y = "Matched hauls") +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),         legend.position  = "top")  p1 / p2

title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere

title: Matching biology to environment: query 75 years of ocean data, anywhere
text: wrappers cover common cases: cc_match_ichthyo_by_name() — filter biology scientific name (vignette). cc_match_ichthyo_by_taxon() — filter WoRMS worms_id every descendant, resolved recursive walk taxon tree GCS. Replaces old API’s ITIS-path-regex pattern. cc_match_zooplankton_biomass() — match net-tow displacement-volume biomass (net.totalplankton / net.smallplankton) bottle measurements. need biological environmental side wrappers don’t cover (custom filter, extra join, different grouping), call core engine cc_match_bio_env(bio, env, ...) directly SELECT subqueries — see ?cc_match_bio_env column contract. broader picture — tables ship release, query hive-partitioned CTD profiles, relationship Integrated App download bundle — see Data Access Matching Helpers chapters CalCOFI documentation book.

title: calcofi4r
text: calcofi4r package provides access CalCOFI (California Cooperative Oceanic Fisheries Investigations) database, contains 75 years oceanographic biological data California Current ecosystem. database organized tidy relational structure stored Parquet files Google Cloud Storage. allows fast queries without downloading entire database.

text: CalCOFI database contains data two main programs: Ichthyoplankton Survey (since 1949): cruise - cruise metadata (691 cruises) ship - research vessels (48 ships) site - sampling locations (61K sites) tow - net tow events (76K tows) net - net samples (77K nets) ichthyo - fish larvae counts (831K records) species - species taxonomy (1,144 species) taxon - taxonomic hierarchy grid - CalCOFI station grid (218 cells) segment - line segments stations Bottle Database (since 1949): casts - CTD/bottle cast events (36K casts) bottle - water sample bottles (895K bottles) bottle_measurement - oceanographic measurements (11M records) measurement_type - measurement definitions (47 types) cast_condition - cast quality flags
code: # show row counts for each table tables <- dbListTables(con) tibble(   table = tables,   rows  = sapply(tables, function(t) {     dbGetQuery(con, sprintf("SELECT COUNT(*) as n FROM %s", t))$n   })) |>   arrange(desc(rows)) #> # A tibble: 28 × 2 #>    table                   rows #>    <chr>                  <dbl> #>  1 ctd_summary        108390249 #>  2 bottle_measurement  11135600 #>  3 ctd_thin             5551551 #>  4 ctd_cast             5550014 #>  5 bottle                895371 #>  6 ichthyo               852228 #>  7 cast_condition        235513 #>  8 net                    76512 #>  9 tow                    75506 #> 10 site                   61104 #> # ℹ 18 more rows

title: calcofi4r
text: bottle data contains oceanographic measurements CTD casts. Measurements stored normalized “long” format bottle_measurement.
code: # get temperature measurements with location and depth d_temp <- dbGetQuery(con, "   SELECT     c.lon_dec as lon,     c.lat_dec as lat,     c.datetime_utc,     b.depth_m,     bm.measurement_value as temperature   FROM bottle_measurement bm   JOIN bottle b ON bm.bottle_id = b.bottle_id   JOIN casts c ON b.cast_id = c.cast_id   WHERE bm.measurement_type = 'temperature'     AND bm.measurement_value IS NOT NULL     AND b.depth_m <= 10   LIMIT 100000")  head(d_temp) #>         lon      lat        datetime_utc depth_m temperature #> 1 -117.4667 33.24167 1975-06-26 13:40:00       0       17.31 #> 2 -117.4667 33.24167 1975-06-26 13:40:00       5       14.78 #> 3 -117.4667 33.24167 1975-06-26 13:40:00      10       13.36 #> 4 -117.4917 33.23333 1975-06-26 15:03:00       0       17.11 #> 5 -117.4917 33.23333 1975-06-26 15:03:00      10       13.01 #> 6 -117.5667 33.19167 1975-06-26 16:21:00       0       17.25 nrow(d_temp) #> [1] 93985

code: # get top 10 species by total count top_species <- dbGetQuery(con, "   SELECT     s.scientific_name,     s.common_name,     SUM(i.tally) as total_count,     COUNT(DISTINCT n.tow_uuid) as n_tows   FROM ichthyo i   JOIN species s ON i.species_id = s.species_id   JOIN net n ON i.net_uuid = n.net_uuid   GROUP BY s.scientific_name, s.common_name   ORDER BY total_count DESC   LIMIT 10")  top_species #>              scientific_name                common_name total_count n_tows #> 1                  Teleostei       Unidentified Teliost     8960182  61416 #> 2           Engraulis mordax           Northern anchovy     8553961  29491 #> 3            Sardinops sagax Pacific sardine (pilchard)     1430924   9766 #> 4       Merluccius productus    Pacific hake or whiting     1298807  12527 #> 5       Vinciguerria lucetia           Panama lightfish      500036  14829 #> 6                   Sebastes                 Rockfishes      289447  18187 #> 7      Trachurus symmetricus              Jack mackerel      260927   9526 #> 8      Leuroglossus stilbius    California smoothtongue      184814  12450 #> 9  Stenobrachius leucopsarus          Northern lampfish      178543  12726 #> 10     Triphoturus mexicanus           Mexican lampfish      147571  14572

title: calcofi4r
text: Map distribution common species:
code: # get Northern Anchovy observations with locations anchovy <- dbGetQuery(con, "   SELECT     si.latitude as lat,     si.longitude as lon,     SUM(i.tally) as count   FROM ichthyo i   JOIN species sp ON i.species_id = sp.species_id   JOIN net n ON i.net_uuid = n.net_uuid   JOIN tow t ON n.tow_uuid = t.tow_uuid   JOIN site si ON t.site_uuid = si.site_uuid   WHERE sp.scientific_name = 'Engraulis mordax'   GROUP BY si.latitude, si.longitude") |>   filter(!is.na(lon), !is.na(lat)) |>   st_as_sf(coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  mapview(anchovy, cex = "count", layer.name = "Anchovy count")

title: calcofi4r
text: View temporal coverage CalCOFI cruises:
code: # get cruise timeline (date_ym is YYYYMM format) cruises <- dbGetQuery(con, "   SELECT     cruise_key,     CAST(SUBSTRING(CAST(date_ym AS VARCHAR), 1, 4) AS INTEGER) as year,     CAST(SUBSTRING(CAST(date_ym AS VARCHAR), 5, 2) AS INTEGER) as month,     ship_key   FROM cruise   WHERE date_ym IS NOT NULL   ORDER BY date_ym")  # count cruises by year cruises |>   count(year) |>   filter(!is.na(year)) |>   ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(year, n)) +   ggplot2::geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +   ggplot2::labs(     title = "CalCOFI Cruises by Year",     x = "Year", y = "Number of Cruises") +   ggplot2::theme_minimal()

what: Generate Mermaid ERD from DuckDB Connection — cc_erd
title: Generate Mermaid ERD from DuckDB Connection — cc_erd
text: Generates Mermaid entity relationship diagram DuckDB connection querying information_schema.columns. Unlike dm::dm_draw(), handles GEOMETRY columns without errors, spatial tables like site, grid, casts, segment included diagram.

title: Generate Mermaid ERD from DuckDB Connection — cc_erd
code: cc_erd(   con,   tables = NULL,   exclude = NULL,   rels_path = NULL,   rels = NULL,   colors = NULL,   layout = "elk",   view_type = "all" )

title: Generate Mermaid ERD from DuckDB Connection — cc_erd
text: con DBI connection DuckDB database tables Character vector table names include. NULL (default), tables connection included. exclude Character vector table names exclude (default: NULL). Applied tables. rels_path Path relationships.json file primary key foreign key definitions. NULL (default), diagram shows table structures without relationship lines. Ignored rels provided. rels list primary_keys (named list: table → column) foreign_keys (list lists table, column, ref_table, ref_column). Alternative rels_path passing relationships inline. Takes precedence rels_path. colors Named list mapping color names hex codes character vectors table names. Emitted Mermaid classDef/class directives color entity outline (stroke) rather fill, multi-row entities stay legible. Build programmatically table → dataset metadata cc_erd_color_map(). Example: list(lightblue = c("cruise", "ship")). layout Layout engine: "elk" (default) "dagre". view_type columns show: "" (default), "keys_only" (PK/FK columns ), "title_only" (table names, columns).

title: Generate Mermaid ERD from DuckDB Connection — cc_erd

what: Match biological to environmental observations — cc_match_bio_env
title: Match biological to environmental observations — cc_match_bio_env
text: Core engine relating biological observations environmental observations matching time space. Builds single DuckDB SQL string — temporal interval join (within max_time_hr) plus spatial filter (ST_Distance_Sphere within max_dist_km) — reads directly Parquet files frozen CalCOFI release Google Cloud Storage, runs , attaches fully-interpolated SQL query metadata attributes.

title: Match biological to environmental observations — cc_match_bio_env
code: cc_match_bio_env(   bio,   env,   max_dist_km = 2,   max_time_hr = 6,   join_method = c("nearest_time", "nearest_dist", "average"),   con = NULL,   version = "latest",   collect = TRUE,   return_sql = FALSE )

title: Match biological to environmental observations — cc_match_bio_env
text: bio SQL SELECT string producing biological side. Must yield columns bio_id (unique per observation), bio_datetime (TIMESTAMP), bio_lon, bio_lat (decimal degrees) bio_value (DOUBLE). additional columns (e.g. scientific_name) carried output grouping keys. env SQL SELECT string producing environmental side. Must yield exactly env_id, env_datetime (TIMESTAMP), env_lon, env_lat, env_value (DOUBLE), env_depth_m (DOUBLE) measurement_type. max_dist_km Maximum match distance kilometers (default: 2). max_time_hr Maximum match time difference hours (default: 6). join_method One "nearest_time" (default — keep env observation(s) closest time, averaging ties), "nearest_dist" (closest space) "average" (average every env observation window). con Optional DuckDB connection. NULL (default) temporary -memory connection created (closed collect = TRUE). version Release version string (e.g. "v2026.05.14") "latest" (default). Recorded query metadata; actual table URLs come bio / env. collect TRUE (default) execute return tibble; FALSE return lazy dplyr::tbl() reference. return_sql TRUE, return interpolated SQL string (query_meta attached) without executing. Default: FALSE.

title: Match biological to environmental observations — cc_match_bio_env
text: return_sql = TRUE, length-1 character vector SQL attr(., "query_meta"). Otherwise tibble (lazy tbl) one row per biological observation per measurement_type, matched env_value, env_value_sd, env_depth_m, n_env, dist_km time_diff_hr, plus bio columns. result carries attr(., "sql") attr(., "query_meta") (package + release version, parameters GCS source URLs).

title: calcofi4r 1.3.0
text: Dataset-driven ERD coloring (stroke-based) cc_erd() stroke-coloring Entity classDefs now color table outline (stroke) instead fill, multi-row entities read cleanly. colors argument unchanged. cc_erd_color_map() New exported helper builds colors list cc_erd() authoritative table → dataset metadata: single-owner tables take dataset color, shared/multi-dataset tables take neutral color, per-table overrides win. Drives dataset-source coloring schema site (calcofi.io/schema).

title: calcofi4r 1.1.10
text: Local data download table filtering cc_get_db() local_data parameter cc_get_db(local_data = TRUE) now downloads parquet files local cache directory creates TABLEs instead remote VIEWs. Files downloaded missing refresh = TRUE, making repeated calls idempotent. tables parameter cc_get_db(tables = c("species", "ichthyo", ...)) filters tables load catalog. Useful excluding large tables like CTD data building app-specific databases.

title: calcofi4r 1.1.9
text: Native GEOMETRY storage cc_get_db() storage_compatibility_version = 'latest' cc_get_db() now uses named DuckDB driver autoload_known_extensions latest storage format, matching calcofi4db’s get_duckdb_con() pattern. Ensures native GEOMETRY type used spatial queries.

title: calcofi4r 1.1.8
text: Require DuckDB >= 1.5.1 native GEOMETRY Requires duckdb >= 1.5.1 Added minimum version constraint ensure native built-GEOMETRY type available spatial queries.

title: calcofi4r 1.1.7
text: ERD diagrams render PNG Quarto via mermaid-cli knit_print.cc_erd() now renders Mermaid diagrams PNG via mmdc (mermaid-cli) 2x scale transparent background, saving knitr::fig_path() proper Quarto HTML output. Lightbox applies automatically via _quarto.yml settings. Fallback chain: mmdc → DiagrammeR::mermaid() htmlwidget → raw mermaid code block.

title: calcofi4r 1.1.6
text: New cc_tbl() unified table accessor cc_tbl() New function providing unified access CalCOFI database table. Returns lazy dplyr::tbl() non-spatial tables, sf object spatial tables (via DuckDB EXCLUDE + ST_AsWKB), pivoted-wide sf _spatial table (requires layer argument). Optional geom_col selects alternate geometry columns (e.g., geom_ctr grid centroids).

title: calcofi4r 1.1.5
